strengths: 15+ years of full-stack experience with deep TypeScript/Node.js proficiency across multiple production environments, directly matching the primary stack
Current role at re:cinq includes hands-on AI agent infrastructure and MCP framework development, directly satisfying the AI agent development requirement
Strong testing culture demonstrated through TDD advocacy, community workshops, and authoring testing libraries — aligns well with the team's quality practices
concerns: NestJS is absent from the resume entirely; given it is a named primary framework, the gap is notable and would require validation
Kotlin/Java experience is only surface-level (robotic arm work at Altom in 2016–2018); no evidence of production Kotlin or modern Java backend work
Vitest and Jest are not explicitly mentioned; testing background is strong in principle but demonstrated primarily through D-language tooling rather than the JS/TS ecosystem
response.justification: Bogdan is a strong technical fit for the core of this role: his TypeScript and Node.js experience is extensive and recent, his current work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling maps directly to the role's AI agent requirement, and his long history of TDD advocacy signals the kind of quality-minded engineering the team values. The primary risk is NestJS — it is a first-class framework in the stack and is completely absent from his resume, so his ramp-up cost there is unknown. The Kotlin/Java gap is a secondary concern given those are listed as secondary technologies. Overall, the candidate covers the most critical dimensions of the role well and the gaps are learnable, making him a credible prospect pending a technical screen that probes NestJS familiarity and JS/TS testing toolchain depth.
